Title: Aizo's unit secures RM346.19mil Islamic financing facilities to support solar project in Kampar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aizo Group Bhd's subsidiary, Wawasan Demi Sdn Bhd, has secured Islamic financing facilities of up to RM346.19 million from Bank Pembangunan Malaysia Bhd (BPMB).
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the facilities will be utilised to support the development of a large-scale solar photovoltaic (LSSPV) plant in Kampar, Perak.
Aizo said the total facilities comprise a Tawarruq Asset Financing (TWA) facility of up to RM256.0 million, alongside bank guarantee facilities totalling RM90.19 million, which will primarily support project execution requirements and contractual obligations under a power purchase agreement (PPA) with Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB).
It added that the TWA facility will be used to part-finance up to 80 per cent of the project's engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning (EPCC) costs.
Meanwhile, the bank guarantee facilities will serve as performance guarantees in favour of TNB for project delivery milestones.
Executive chairman Datuk Abang Abdillah Izzarim said the financing facilities represent a key milestone in advancing Aizo's renewable energy (RE) initiatives.
He said the Kampar LSSPV project is a strategic asset that will contribute to Aizo's long-term growth, while supporting Malaysia's transition towards sustainable energy.
"With a substantial portion of the project cost now secured, we are well-positioned to accelerate implementation and execution.
"The development also strengthens Aizo's recurring income potential through long-term power generation under the TNB power purchase framework," he said.
Upon completion and commissioning, the solar plant is expected to provide stable and predictable income streams, underpinned by long-term contractual arrangements.
Looking ahead, Aizo remains focused on executing the Kampar solar project efficiently while continuing to explore opportunities within the RE and infrastructure space, in line with national energy transition policies and growing demand for clean power solutions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorm warning for nine states, Labuan until 9pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning for nine states and Labuan until 9pm today.
MetMalaysia, in a statement today, said the warning covers the whole of Perlis, as well as Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Sik and Baling) and Perak (Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar).
Similar weather conditions are also forecast in Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Bentong, Bera and Rompin), Selangor (Hulu Langat), Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu and Jempol) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang and Mersing).
In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching (Bau and Kuching), Serian, Samarahan (Samarahan and Asajaya), Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu, Mukah (Tanjung Manis), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Bintulu (Tatau and Sebauh), Miri (Marudi) and Limbang.
The same warning has also been issued for Sabah, involving the interior (Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan), the west coast, and Sandakan (Tongod and Kinabatangan). – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nancy Shukri's husband dies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The husband of Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri died today.
Datuk Kamil Misuari, a Sabahan businessman, died in Kuala Lumpur. He will be laid to rest in Sarawak tomorrow, Nancy's office said in a statement.
"Members of the public may pay their last respects at Nancy's residence in Taman Serira, Jalan Menggeris, Petra Jaya, Kuching, before the burial at the Samariang Islamic Cemetery in Kuching," the statement added.
"Let us pray that the late Datuk Kamil's soul will be placed among the faithful.
"Al-Fatihah."
Kamil and Nancy have three children together; Faeroz, Farouk and Bibi.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu's use of PN logo: Only coalition chairman can authorise this, says Pas
Article:
KOTA BARU: The rift within Perikatan Nasional continued to grow yesterday as Pas leaders questioned whether Bersatu could use the coalition's logo in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan elections.
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man and Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah both said only the PN chairman can authorise use of the logo for any election.
This practice, said Tuan Ibrahim, was followed in previous elections when Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin was PN chairman.
He said it was Muhyiddin who issued and signed PN authorisation letters.
"During the last general election, Tan Sri Muhyiddin signed the PN authorisation letter, while party authorisation letters were signed by the president or secretary-general of the respective parties," said the Pas vice president.
Tuan Ibrahim was commenting on Muhyiddin's announcement that Bersatu will remain in PN and contest the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections
using the coalition's logo
.
Amar said he believed Muhyiddin may have overlooked PN's procedures when making the announcement.
He agreed with Tuan Ibrahim that only the PN chairman has the authority to approve the use of the coalition's logo and that such matters should first be discussed and endorsed at the coalition level.
"The authorisation letter is signed by the PN chairman, not by the president of any individual component party.
"Perhaps Muhyiddin forgot and thought the logo could be used so easily in Johor and Negeri Sembilan.
"There are rules and procedures. He should not have made the announcement prematurely," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Swift Energy secures RM44.15mil solar, data centre contracts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Swift Energy Technology Bhd has secured two separate purchase orders worth a combined total of RM44.148 million through its wholly-owned subsidiary, Swift Energy Sdn Bhd.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the company said the first contract, awarded by Offshore Oil Engineering Co Ltd, involves the supply of 18 sets of Ex Solar Power Systems.
These systems are slated for the PTTEP Bundled Phases 4 Project located in Thailand, with delivery expected to take place over the next three years.
Swift Energy Technology said the second contract was secured from Cummins Sales and Service Sdn Bhd.
Under this agreement, the company will supply 34 sets of 11 kV Cressall Neutral Earthing Resistors for a data centre project in Malaysia, with delivery expected by January 2027.
It stated that both contracts are expected to contribute positively to the company's earnings and net assets for the financial year ending Sept 30, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cop charged with possessing drugs in court lock-up
Article:
KOTA BARU: A police corporal pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to possessing 19.8g of methamphetamine in the lock-up of the Kota Baru courts complex last week.
Mohammad Fadhirul Musa, 38, made the plea before magistrate Rais Imran Hamid.
He was charged with possessing 19.8g gross of methamphetamine at the lock-up of the courts complex at 8.29am on June 8.
The charge, framed under Section 12(2) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and punishable under Section 39A(1) of the same Act, carries a jail term of up to five years and a maximum of nine strokes of the cane upon conviction.
Prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutor Muhamad Nadzir Abdullah, while the accused was represented by lawyers Mohd Azrizal Hamzi and Muhammad Husni Mubarak Ghazali.
The court allowed bail at RM3,500 with one surety and fixed July 30 for mention.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Binastra's Q1 profit climbs 40pct as new construction services gain traction
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Binastra Corp Bhd's net profit jumped 40 per cent in the first quarter of financial year ending Jan 31, 2027 (Q1 FY27), driven by its expansion into new construction services.
The net profit grew to RM35.27 million from RM25 million a year ago.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing, the company said its construction segment, the primary contributor to revenue, benefited from its expansion into solar installation projects, alongside higher contributions from conventional construction and data centre projects.
Revenue for the quarter ended April 30, 2026 more than doubled to RM605.6 million from RM256.8 million a year earlier.
In a statement, Binastra managing director Jackson Tan Kak Seng said the company's strong start to FY27 reflects the resilience of its diversified project portfolio and on successful execution of its growth strategy.
"The expansion into solar installation projects has enhanced our earnings base, while our core construction and data centre projects continue to deliver steady contributions.
"While the operating environment remains dynamic amid fluctuations in material prices, we remain focused on disciplined project execution, prudent cost management and operational efficiency.
"These priorities will enable us to navigate challenges effectively while continuing to deliver value to our clients and shareholders," he said.
Backed by an outstanding order book of about RM6.8 billion and RM819.5 million in new contracts secured to date in FY27, Binastra said it remains on solid footing to sustain earnings visibility and long-term expansion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to table tougher laws against 'tontos', illegal racers next week
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Transport Ministry will table new amendments to the Road Transport Act during the upcoming Dewan Rakyat sitting next week, which will introduce punishments for illegal racing and "tontos" (syndicate lookouts), alongside higher fine rates for serious traffic offences.
The term "tonto", a colloquial abbreviation for "tolong orang nak tipu orang," refers to middlemen who track enforcement officers and tip off illegal syndicates.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said that the upcoming bill involves 11 scopes covering 42 clauses.
This marks the first major overhaul of the Act since 2020, when penalties for drink-driving were heavily increased.
"This is the first amendment to be brought this year, and another amendment is expected to be tabled during the parliamentary session at the end of the year," Loke said during a special media briefing at the Railway Assets Corporation (RAC) headquarters today.
"The second amendment later this year will focus on a proposed compensation mechanism for accident victims involving drink-drivers, motorists testing positive for drugs, or reckless driving to ensure justice for victims and their families," he added.
For the immediate amendments kicking off next week, Loke said a primary objective is to empower the digital transformation of the Road Transport Department (RTD) to reduce reliance on conventional, manual enforcement methods.
The integration of technology, such as the Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) system, will allow officers to automatically detect road tax status, insurance validity, and vehicle records without needing to physically halt traffic.
"We want enforcement that is smarter, more efficient, and reduces direct interaction between officers and road users.
"This approach not only increases effectiveness but also mitigates the negative perceptions and integrity issues often associated with physical roadblocks," Loke said.
Turning to enforcement disruptions, Loke added that the law needs sharper teeth to deal with illegal street racers and tonto syndicates that actively obstruct RTD personnel.
"We want clearer legal provisions so that criminal action can be taken against any party that disrupts, tails, or threatens enforcement officers during their duties," he said.
On the controversial proposal to raise the maximum compound rate from the current RM300 ceiling, Loke said that the hike would not apply across the board, but would target specific, serious, and repeated offences where the existing fines no longer act as a deterrent.
However, road users will have time to adapt.
"If approved, implementation will not happen immediately.
"We are looking at a transition period of at least two years before the new fine rates are enforced to ensure the public can adjust and the rollout is orderly," he said.
The bill is scheduled for its first reading on Monday, with the debate and second reading taking place the following day.
Loke said that the proposed changes have already been thoroughly vetted by the Parliamentary Special Select Committee, that include bipartisan input from both government and opposition lawmakers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMA: Malaysia at risk of losing specialist doctors
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Doctors should not be penalised for undertaking government-recognised training while continuing to serve in the public healthcare system, said the Malaysian Medical Association (MMA).
Its president, Datuk Dr R. Thirunavukarasu, said the government, which sponsors and recognises such training, should also acknowledge the service provided during that period.
"MMA is particularly concerned about the Annual Performance Appraisal Report (LNPT) requirement for the 2026/2027 intake, which shifted from the best three appraisals out of five years to three consecutive years. Such changes should be clearly communicated and implemented with adequate notice.
"One of the key issues involves specialists undergoing recognised Masters training. These doctors continue serving patients while training, yet their training years are often recorded as
cuti belajar
, resulting in gaps in LNPT records. This makes little sense," he said in a statement.
Dr Thirunavukarasu said 70 per cent of Malaysians depend on the public healthcare system for their care.
"When we lose a subspecialist, it is the public patient who pays the price through longer waiting times, delayed treatment and reduced access to specialised care.
"Malaysia is facing a critical shortage of specialists. The government itself has acknowledged a shortfall of nearly 11,000 specialists. At a time when the country is working to close this gap, we should be removing barriers to specialist training, not creating new ones."
Dr Thirunavukarasu said Malaysia needs more subspecialists in areas such as cardiology, oncology, gastroenterology and neonatology, yet specialists seeking subspecialty training are facing increasing uncertainty over eligibility requirements.
The subspecialty programme is overseen by the Health Ministry, but eligibility rules and LNPT requirements fall under the Public Service Department.
"This means the ministry responsible for developing the healthcare workforce does not fully control the rules governing progression into subspecialty training."
Under the current system, a doctor may require more than 15 years to progress from house officer to subspecialist.
"Every additional delay shortens the number of productive years they can serve the nation as a subspecialist. Every delayed subspecialist means one fewer future cardiologist, oncologist, gastroenterologist or neonatologist available to serve patients," said Dr Thirunavukarasu.
He also expressed concern that applicants are often informed only that they were unsuccessful without any explanation.
"Decisions that affect careers and ultimately the country's future specialist workforce should be transparent. Written reasons and a proper appeal mechanism should be made available," he said.
To address these issues, MMA called for clear written clarification of LNPT requirements and eligibility criteria; recognition of service performed during recognised training years; standardised eligibility requirements across recognised pathways; written reasons and a proper appeal mechanism for unsuccessful applicants; and, greater emphasis on competency-based selection.
"The solution is straightforward and does not require another task force. The ministry, PSD and the Higher Education Ministry must work together to ensure that workforce policies support, rather than hinder, the development of future subspecialists.
"Malaysia needs more subspecialists, not fewer. A specialist blocked at home is a specialist recruited abroad. And the patient left behind is in a public hospital."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 58 held in crackdown on World Cup betting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested 58 people, including four women, for illegal sports betting and online gambling activities linked to the 2026 World Cup.
Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the arrests were made during 52 raids conducted nationwide under Op Soga XI.
He said police seized RM25,684.65 in cash and uncovered betting transactions valued at RM488,582.47.
"In addition, 42 websites, platforms, applications and social media accounts were identified as being used for football betting activities and the promotion of gambling services. Action to block the websites and remove the content will be taken with the assistance of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC)," he said in a statement.
Op Soga XI is being conducted from June 11 to July 19 in collaboration with various agencies, particularly MCMC, to monitor gambling-related promotions and advertisements on websites and social media platforms.
Kumar said all suspects had been referred to the relevant district police headquarters for further investigation.
He said investigations are being conducted under Sections 6(1) and 6(3) of the Betting Act 1953, Section 4(1)(c) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 and the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
"Members of the public are urged to provide information to assist the authorities in curbing such activities," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 18, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today, supported by improving global risk sentiment after US President Donald Trump signed a preliminary agreement aimed at ending the conflict in the Middle East.
Investor sentiment was further lifted by a sharp decline in crude oil prices, with Brent crude briefly touching its lowest level since the conflict began.
Meanwhile, the ringgit weakened against the US dollar, closing at 4.1120.
In the crypto market, Bitcoin eased to RM265,086.
Ethereum and Solana also traded lower at RM7,194 and RM295, respectively.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Study finds ex-servicemen wardens cut bullying, initiative expands to all 58 MRSM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will continue recruiting full-time wardens from among former military personnel for all 58 Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) nationwide.
Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the move followed findings of a study which confirmed the effectiveness of a pilot project involving full-time former servicemen as wardens at the Besut and Balik Pulau MRSM.
He said the findings showed a drastic reduction in student disciplinary issues, while bullying cases were almost reduced to zero during the wardens' deployment at the schools.
The report was prepared by Universiti Putra Malaysia's Institute for Social Science Studies and handed over by its lead researcher, Dr Haliza Abdul Rahman.
"Based on the positive findings of this study, the Mara Council will implement the recruitment of full-time wardens from among former military personnel at all 58 MRSM nationwide.
"I have also asked UPM to work together in preparing training modules for these new wardens and to extend the study to address the issue of psychological bullying in a holistic manner," he said in a Facebook post today.
He said among the key findings of the report were that former military personnel were effective in managing discipline and physical security at hostels.
He said the report also recorded positive acceptance from students, parents and administrators.
The study found that the wardens' regular and systematic presence reduced opportunities for bullying and vandalism, while making students feel safer and more disciplined in observing schedules, maintaining personal appearance and keeping hostel areas clean.
It also found that the wardens helped instil values of respect and good manners, while strengthening students' spiritual development through their direct involvement in religious activities.
Asyraf said the appointment of full-time wardens had also reduced the burden on teachers, allowing them to focus more on academic excellence and character-building.
On April 20, Asyraf said all MRSMs would have full-time wardens from among former military personnel starting next year.
He said 18 MRSM schools would begin implementing the warden service this month, before it is expanded to all next year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit closes lower against greenback on Fed rate hike expectations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Thursday as expectations of a possible US Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate hike later this year boosted demand for the greenback.
At 6\pm, the local note fell to 4.1145/1195 against the greenback from 4.0665/0700 at Tuesday's close.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said expectations of a 25-basis-point interest rate hike by the Fed this year continued to support the US dollar.
"This is despite the Fed's forecast for lower federal fund rates of 3.6 per cent in 2027 and 3.4 per cent in 2028, compared with 3.8 per cent in 2026.
"Hence, we believe there appears to be a knee-jerk reaction among traders as the Fed is also abandoning forward guidance in its accompanying statement," he told Bernama.
At the close, the ringgit was mostly lower against a basket of major currencies.
It depreciated against the Japanese yen to 2.5588/5620 from 2.5360/5384 at Tuesday's close and slipped versus the euro to 4.7222/7280 from 4.7175/7216. However, it strengthened against the British pound to 5.4476/4542 from 5.4552/4599 previously.
Against regional currencies, the local note was lower.
It weakened against the Thai baht to 12.5611/5809 from 12.5027/5192 at Tuesday's close, depreciated versus the Indonesian rupiah to 231.2/231.5 from 229.3/229.7 previously, eased against the Philippine peso to 6.79/6.80 from 6.74/6.75 and declined against the Singapore dollar to 3.1898/1939 from 3.1722/1752.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Buying in consumer stocks helps Bursa Malaysia close slightly higher
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's key index finished marginally higher, supported by strong buying interest in consumer-related counters, amid mixed performance across regional markets.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose by 1.40 points, or 0.08 per cent, to 1,711.39 from Tuesday's close of 1,709.99.
The key index opened 12.36 points firmer at 1,722.35 and moved between 1,711.31 and 1,722.63 throughout the session.
Market breadth was negative, with losers leading gainers 678 to 493, while 549 counters were unchanged, 1,016 untraded and 34 suspended.
Turnover increased to 4.50 billion units worth RM3.45 billion from 3.93 billion units worth RM3.45 billion on Tuesday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said bargain hunting continued to emerge in selected blue-chip counters, particularly those with resilient earnings prospects and attractive valuations.
Nevertheless, he said investors are likely to remain selective as the peace agreement has yet to be formally finalised, while uncertainties surrounding the global economic outlook and foreign fund flows remain key considerations.
"Overall, we maintain a cautiously optimistic stance on the local market, with the FBM KLCI expected to trade within the 1,700-1,720 range towards the weekend," he told Bernama.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said today's positive performance was supported by improving global risk sentiment after United States President Donald Trump signed a preliminary agreement aimed at ending the conflict in Middle East.
He said the development fuelled optimism that tensions between the US and Iran could ease further, helping restore confidence across regional equity markets.
"Looking ahead, investors will continue to monitor developments surrounding the Middle East peace process, movements in crude oil prices, and upcoming global economic data releases for further direction.
"Sustained easing in geopolitical risks could provide additional support for risk assets, particularly sectors that benefit from lower energy and transportation costs," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GIIB founder returns as chairman after MACC clearance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: GIIB Holdings Bhd has appointed its founder Tai Boon Wee as non-executive chairman.
This marks the completion of a planned leadership transition following his clearance from an investigation by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC).
Tai, who led the company to its initial public offering in 2004, had earlier stepped down from his executive and board roles to allow investigations to proceed without any perception of corporate interference.
The company said outgoing chairman Datuk Seri Wan Adnan Wan Mamat had relinquished the role after overseeing the group during the transition period.
"When I stepped into the role of chairman, my primary mission was to restore stability and protect shareholder value while legal processes took their course," Wan Adnan said.
"Now that the company has weathered through the turbulent time and regained its footing, it is only right that the leadership of GIIB returns to its founder."
Tai thanked Wan Adnan for steering the company during a challenging period and welcomed newly-appointed board members Koh Seok Keng and Ng Chee Keong.
"I want to express my deepest appreciation to Datuk Seri Wan Adnan for his exemplary leadership during the challenging period for the company and its subsidiaries," he said.
"With the new board composition, I believe the company is poised for growth and success while ensuring good corporate governance."
GIIB said the board unanimously agreed that the timing was appropriate for Tai to resume the chairmanship following the conclusion of the MACC investigation and the strengthening of the group's governance framework.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: El Power secures RM90.1mil data centre subcontract in Johor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Power engineering solutions provider EI Power Bhd's subsidiary EI Power Technologies Sdn Bhd has bagged a RM90.1 million subcontract for a data centre project in Johor.
According to a bourse filing, the works subcontract agreement was awarded by a main contractor involved in engineering, construction and investment holding.
The contractor's identity was not disclosed due to confidentiality provisions under the agreement.
The contract covers the complete fuel system package for the data centre, including the supply, delivery, installation, testing and commissioning of the system.
Work has started upon acceptance of the subcontract on June 18 and is expected to be completed by September 2027.
In a statement, EI Power said its unbilled order book has increased to RM222.5 million, adding to its revenue pipeline and is expected to contribute positively to the earnings over the duration of the project.
Executive director and chief executive officer Ir Albert Chang Wan Siong said the contract underscores the growing recognition of the company's capabilities in mission-critical power engineering at the highest level.
Chang said the award, secured just weeks after the company's listing, reflects the growth trajectory it had set out to deliver for its shareholders.
"Data centres remain one of the most exciting and structurally supported growth segments in Malaysia.
"This contract is a clear reflection of the opportunities available to us, and we intend to build on this momentum to win further contracts in data centres, renewable energy and other infrastructure segments that are shaping Malaysia's growth story," he added.
EI Power was listed on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia on May 21, raising RM62.2 million in proceeds to fund operational expansion, including a new headquarters, a branch office in Johor and its foray into Thailand.
It serves a broad range of industries including data centres, commercial, industrial and residential developments, as well as solar farms.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wanita MCA calls for better childcare, job access for single mothers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Wanita MCA has called for stronger policy for single mothers, citing a rise in their numbers and growing challenges posed by the cost of living.
Its chief Datuk Wong You Fong said current aid programmes, while welcomed, were insufficient to address the structural challenges faced by single mothers.
She said figures released by the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry showed the number of single mothers had more than doubled since 2000, with Selangor, Johor and Perak recording the highest numbers.
"Support for single mothers must be treated as a matter of national economic and social policy, not merely welfare administration," she said in a statement today.
While acknowledging the government's efforts, including the RM3.1 billion allocation to the Welfare Department and the Kasih Ibu Tunggal (Kasihnita) programme, Wong said more comprehensive measures were needed to provide long-term support.
She said access to affordable childcare should be prioritised to ensure single mothers are not excluded from the workforce due to caregiving responsibilities.
"The government must expand the availability of subsidised and affordable childcare centres, particularly in urban and low-income communities," she said.
Wong also called for stronger employment policies to improve labour market access for single mothers through structured job placement mechanisms, enhanced career-matching systems and closer collaboration with the private sector.
At the same time, she said employers should be encouraged to adopt flexible and family-friendly working arrangements, where feasible, to support workforce participation.
In addition, Wong urged the government to strengthen mental health support by expanding access to counselling services, peer support networks and parenting programmes at community level.
She said single mothers often shoulder multiple responsibilities, including earning an income, caring for children, managing households and providing emotional support, frequently without adequate support systems.
"A coordinated policy response involving expanded childcare infrastructure, improved labour market access and stronger mental health support systems is needed to help single mothers achieve sustainable economic independence and social stability," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Women key to AI transformation, says Nancy Shukri
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Women must play a central role in shaping the future of artificial intelligence (AI) rather than merely adapting to technological change, said Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri.
Speaking at the AI SHE LEADS forum, held in conjunction with the Kuala Lumpur International PR Conference (KLIP 8), Nancy stressed that AI development must remain grounded in people and values.
"Technology is not just about machines. It is about people. It is about our values, our communities and our future," she said.
She added that women should be active participants in driving technological change.
"Women should not only adapt, but women must also help shape the direction of technology," she said.
Drawing on examples from Sarawak, Nancy said AI has the potential to expand opportunities for women entrepreneurs regardless of location.
"With AI, even a small business owner in Kapit or Betong can reach customers far beyond her district," she said.
She also underscored the need for responsible adoption of emerging technologies, noting that "ethical AI is not just a technical matter. It is a social responsibility".
More than 300 executives, policymakers and communication professionals from Malaysia and across the Asia-Pacific attended the forum, which examined the role of AI in leadership, governance and digital inclusion.
Director-general of Higher Education Ministry Professor Datuk Dr Azlinda Azman highlighted the ministry's AI-powered virtual counsellor, AIMI, as an example of how technology can be used to support student wellbeing and mental health.
Meanwhile, forum host Jaffri Amin Osman said qualities such as empathy, trust-building and authentic communication remain essential in leadership despite rapid technological advances.
"While AI may transform how we work, it is human character that ultimately defines how we lead with wisdom, purpose and impact," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KHPT lands RM155mil Proton deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: KHPT Holdings Bhd's unit Automev Global Sdn Bhd has secured a contract from Proton Holdings Bhd's unit Perusahaan Otomobil Nasional Sdn Bhd to manufacture and supply selected automotive structural components for a new model.
In a bourse filing, KHPT said the letter of acceptance (LOA) is expected to generate about RM155 million in revenue over the lifespan of a new vehicle model.
"The LOA shall remain valid throughout the model life of the vehicle, unless otherwise extended or terminated in accordance with its terms and conditions," it said.
KHPT said the contract will not have any effect on its share capital or shareholding structure and is not expected to materially impact its net assets for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026.
It is expected to contribute positively to the company's earnings over the duration of the contract.
However, KHPT said it may utilise bank borrowings and internally generated funds to support the execution of the contract, which could result in higher gearing levels where applicable.
In a statement, KHPT said the production will be carried out at the manufacturing facility in Telok Panglima Garang, Selangor.
The LOA reflects the company's continued participation in the domestic automotive supply chain and reinforces its long-standing business relationship with Proton.
Group managing director Datin Eloise See said the contract reflects the company's technical capabilities, manufacturing discipline and commitment to consistently meeting customer requirements.
"We remain focused on operational execution, quality and supporting the long-term development of Malaysia's automotive supply chain," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teen robbed, raped during morning jog in Tumpat
Article:
TUMPAT: A 15-year-old girl on a morning jog with her younger brother in Palekbang, here, was robbed and raped by a man yesterday.
The teenager was found injured near a bush by a passer-by about 11am and was taken to hospital for treatment.
Her grandmother said the victim had left home with her brother for exercise earlier in the morning.
She said the boy became separated from his sister during the jog and later returned home after failing to locate her.
"My grandson told me he became tired and was left behind while jogging. He searched for his sister but could not find her.
"Thinking she might have returned home, he went back by himself," said the grandmother.
She said she later received a call from the victim's father about noon informing her that the teenager had been found near a bush.
"The news shocked all of us. I cannot imagine what happened to my granddaughter.
"She is a good child and very obedient to her parents. I hope those responsible will face justice," she said.
The victim is currently receiving treatment at Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital in Kota Baru.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Khairi Shafie said the case is being investigated under Section 376 of the Penal Code for rape, which carries a jail term of up to 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Road rage cleaver attacker jailed after failing to post RM15,000 bail
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A 43-year-old salesman who pleaded guilty to attacking a fellow motorcyclist with a meat cleaver will spend the night in jail after failing to post RM15,000 bail.
Azmi Jantan pleaded guilty
earlier today at the Sessions Court to intentionally causing grievous hurt to Mohd Fairus Jaafar, a 45-year-old leasing executive, by slashing him with a meat cleaver on June 11.
The charge sheet described the meat cleaver as a weapon capable of causing death.
The incident occurred at 8.19am in the motorcycle lane at Km14.6 of the Federal Highway in Section 13.
Sources told the New Straits Times that as of 4pm today, none of Azmi's next of kin had appeared in court to post bail.
He was then taken to Sungai Buloh Prison, where he will remain until he is able to post bail or until his next court date.
Sessions Court Judge Datin Fatimah Zahari set July 8 for the presentation of facts and sentencing.
The offence, framed under Section 326 of the Penal Code, carries a maximum 20-year jail term, a fine, or whipping upon conviction.
Azmi, who was unrepresented, had pleaded for a lower bail of RM5,000.
This was significantly less than deputy public prosecutor Ho Zee Xing's initial request of RM30,000 with one surety.
While Judge Fatimah ultimately settled on RM15,000, she ordered that Azmi must also report to the nearest police station once a month as an additional bail condition.
The attack took place when the victim was riding to work towards Kuala Lumpur.
As Fairus was passing through the motorcycle lane, he honked at another rider who was allegedly obstructing his path.
Angered by the gesture, the accused stopped by the roadside and repeatedly slashed Fairus with a meat cleaver.
In a Facebook post,
Fairus described the horrific ordeal
as something he never imagined experiencing, calling it the kind of violence usually seen only in crime dramas.
He also shared several photographs showing extensive stitches on his hand, arm, forehead, and shoulder.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Generali unveils its Asia first education initiative in Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Italian insurer Generali Malaysia has launched Gen Sinar personal accident insurance plan aimed at helping underserved families protect their children's education against unexpected financial hardship.
Developed in partnership with Amanah Raya Bhd, the initiative marks Generali's first education-focused social protection programme in Asia.
Generali Malaysia chief executive officer Fabrice Benard said the initiative is designed to create social impact rather than generate commercial returns.
"By making protection accessible, we aim to ensure children can continue their education even in the most difficult circumstances," he said at the launch today.
Benard said Generali, AmanahRaya and its distribution partners have waived profits to keep the plan affordable for lower-income households.
The insurer will distribute Gen Sinar through its nationwide network of nearly 7,000 agents.
He acknowledged that reaching the B40 segment remains a challenge, noting that affordable insurance products are often difficult to market despite strong public interest.
As such, Benard said the company has not set sales targets, choosing instead to focus on expanding financial protection for underserved communities.
He said AmanahRaya was selected as trustee because of its established reputation and experience in safeguarding beneficiaries' interests.
"When we looked at the market, only AmanahRaya was deemed fit because we needed a trusted institution to safeguard children's education funds," said Benard.
The plan targets households with limited financial protection, particularly those dependent on a single breadwinner.
Under the arrangement, AmanahRaya will administer the education benefits as trustee to ensure the funds are managed solely for children's education.
AmanahRaya group chief business and operating officer Jack Yap Ngee Heong said the organisation's role is to safeguard the trust and ensure the education benefits reach the intended beneficiaries.
"The product belongs to Generali. We are the trustee to ensure the education benefits are properly managed for the children.
"The collaboration reflects a shared commitment to improving financial inclusion and supporting underserved communities," he said.
Yap added that AmanahRaya is open to similar partnerships with organisations pursuing initiatives that benefit Malaysians.
The plan provides a RM20,000 lump sum payment upon the accidental death or permanent disability of the breadwinner.
It also offers a monthly education allowance of RM250 per child until the age of 18, with total education support of up to RM100,000.
Policyholders will also receive two complimentary telemedicine consultations annually, valued at up to RM60 per session, covering both physical and mental health.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suit against Muhyiddin: Court slashes Guan Eng's damages from RM1.05mil to RM450k
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal today reduced the general damages awarded to DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng from RM1.05 million to RM450,000 in a defamation suit filed against Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin over statements relating to the revocation of tax exemption for Yayasan Albukhary.
A three-member panel comprising Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid and Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar made the ruling after allowing part of Muhyiddin's appeal against the Kuala Lumpur High Court's decision.
In the unanimous verdict, Justice Azhahari said that after examining the three impugned statements dated March 9, 11 and 12, 2023, the High Court was correct in finding them defamatory and there was no reason to disturb that finding of fact.
"On the issue of quantum, having considered the pattern of events and the evidence adduced at trial, we are of the opinion that the general damages awarded are manifestly high.
"Accordingly, this court awards RM150,000 for each of the three statements, amounting to RM450,000. We therefore substitute the sum of RM1.05 million with RM450,000," he said.
The panel also set aside the High Court's award of RM150,000 in aggravated damages and RM150,000 in exemplary damages to Lim.
However, the court allowed Lim's cross-appeal on interest and ordered that interest be paid at the rate of five per cent per annum from the date of judgment until full settlement.
The panel ordered Muhyiddin to pay RM5,000 in costs to Lim, while the former Penang chief minister was ordered to pay RM30,000 in costs to the former prime minister.
On March 27, 2023, Lim filed the lawsuit against Muhyiddin for allegedly making three defamatory statements on Facebook on March 9, 11 and 12, 2023, linking him to the tax exemption issue.
Reports on the matter were subsequently published by several newspapers and news portals.
In Nov 2024, the High Court allowed Lim's suit and ordered Muhyiddin to pay RM350,000 for each of the three statements, amounting to RM1.05 million in general damages, as well as RM150,000 in aggravated damages and RM150,000 in exemplary damages.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Beyond crises: Engineering Malaysia's future well-being
Article:
LETTERS:
Recent events reminded Malaysians of the role engineering plays in everyday life. Incidents like floods, Putra Heights gas pipeline explosion and more recently, fatalities at a Floating Storage and Offloading oil and gas facility share a common denominator: engineering.
Floods should not be discussed solely as drainage issues, while infrastructure failures are not merely operational failures and industrial accidents are not simply workplace incidents.
The importance of engineering is reflected in Malaysia's economic structure. High-technology exports account for 59.42 per cent of manufactured exports.
In January 2026, manufactured products contributed 86.7 per cent of total exports, with E&E products alone accounting for 48 per cent (RM70.5 billion) of national exports.
Malaysia is also the world's sixth-largest semiconductor exporter, supporting 260,000 jobs, generating US$95.6 billion in exports in 2024 and contributing seven per cent of global semiconductor trade.
Under the National Semiconductor Strategy, Malaysia is targeting RM500 billion in investments while developing local chip design capabilities and upskilling 60,000 engineers.
However, achieving these ambitions requires more than capital.
Global competition in semiconductors, for instance, is a competition for talent and innovation rather than investment alone.
The challenge is whether technical expertise is systematically incorporated into national planning before problems emerge and opportunities are missed.
Nations that invest consistently in engineering capability are often better prepared to withstand crises. Iran demonstrates how local technical talent and innovation can strengthen national resilience despite prolonged external pressures.
Malaysia faces a similar challenge, where from flood resilience and infrastructure safety to food security and semiconductor competitiveness, engineering connects policy with implementation.
Yet, engineering perspectives are frequently sought only after policies have been formulated, projects have commenced or crises have occurred.
Unlike economics, public health and national security, engineering lacks a dedicated platform through which multidisciplinary expertise can inform national policy.
Thus, who speaks for engineering when decisions on infrastructure, energy transition, semiconductors, climate adaptation and emerging technologies are made?
The issue is not a lack of expertise, but whether it is fully utilised in national policymaking.
A national platform would facilitate coordination and ensure technical considerations are incorporated earlier into policymaking. It would also allow Malaysia to leverage its engineering talent in addressing complex national challenges.
This becomes even more urgent as global semiconductor revenues are projected to exceed US$1 trillion within the next decade.
Climate change is accelerating faster than predicted which requires new adaptation strategies.
AI is reshaping industries and public services at a pace speedier than the Industrial Revolution.
Food and water security will depend on technological innovation and resource optimisation as traditional agricultural cannot meet the demands of 68 per cent increase in the global urban population by 2050.
At the same time, nations are competing aggressively for engineering talent, advanced manufacturing capabilities and technological leadership.
Malaysia's future competitiveness depends on how effectively it develops, coordinates and deploys its engineering capacity.
As we aspire to become a technology creator rather than merely a technology user, engineering must be recognised as a strategic national capability.
Our future will be determined by the technologies we adopt and the ability to transform knowledge into solutions and ambition into action.
As Malaysia advances towards becoming a high-income, tech-driven economy between 2028 and 2030, the role of engineering extends beyond responding to crises.
ASSOCIATE PROFESSOR DR SYUHAIDA ISMAIL
Associate Professor
Malaysia–Japan International Institute of Technology
Universiti Teknologi Malaysia
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Dialogue, diplomacy key to lasting peace amid global tensions
Article:
KAZAN: Malaysia has emphasised that dialogue and diplomacy must remain the foundation for addressing conflicts and geopolitical tensions in an increasingly uncertain world, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"Malaysia remains firmly committed to the principle that lasting peace can only be achieved through negotiations, deeper understanding and strong respect for international law," he said in Malaysia's statement at the 35th Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit here today.
Beyond geopolitical issues, Anwar said the summit also focused on efforts to strengthen economic and technological cooperation for the mutual benefit of Asean and Russia.
He said key strategic sectors identified included trade, investment, the digital economy, science and technology, artificial intelligence, energy, food security, the halal industry and people-to-people ties.
"These sectors are capable of generating greater impact and benefits for all Asean member states and the Russian Federation," he said.
On developments in the Middle East, he reiterated Malaysia's firm call for an immediate end to violence in Gaza.
He said Malaysia also demanded the unhindered delivery of humanitarian aid and reaffirmed its consistent position in defending the inalienable right of the Palestinian people to determine their own future and sovereignty.
"Malaysia also strongly condemns the expansion of military action by the Zionist Israeli regime into Lebanon.
"The aggressive actions of the regime, including attacks targeting the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon, are also strongly condemned as they clearly violate international law and the United Nations Charter."
Anwar said Malaysia believed Asean and Russia had wide room to further strengthen existing cooperation based on mutual respect, shared benefits and a firm commitment to global peace, stability and prosperity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asean, Russia renew 35-year strategic partnership commitment
Article:
KAZAN: Asean and Russia have renewed their commitment to strengthening a strategic partnership built over 35 years on mutual respect, sovereign equality and multilateral cooperation.
The commitment was made at the 35th Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit here today, which was co-chaired by Russia and the Philippines, as Asean chair.
Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr said ties that began in Kuala Lumpur more than three decades ago had matured into a partnership that has delivered tangible impact and cooperation for both sides.
He said the principles that guided the early phase of Asean-Russia relations, as enshrined in the Treaty of Amity and Cooperation, remained relevant and continued to serve as a compass in navigating the current period of global uncertainty.
Marcos outlined three strategic priorities for both sides moving forward, namely, peace and stability, a more dynamic economic partnership and stronger people-to-people ties.
"In an era of deepening geopolitical uncertainty, the value of steady political and security engagement between Asean and Russia cannot be underestimated.
"Transnational threats such as terrorism, illicit trafficking, cybercrime and online scams do not respect borders, and neither should our responses," he said in his opening remarks at the summit's plenary session.
Marcos urged regional institutions to strengthen cooperation in maritime security and counter-terrorism, while building institutional capacity that is anticipatory rather than merely reactive.
On the economic front, Marcos said trade and investment ties between both sides had grown but had yet to reach their full potential.
He called for stronger trade facilitation, deeper investment flows and closer links between business communities, with particular attention to food and energy security.
Marcos also stressed the need to invest in education, scholarships, culture and tourism, with young people placed at the centre of efforts to shape the future of Asean-Russia relations.
"The ties forged among our people will outlast any summit outcome and will carry our partnership forward in ways that policy alone cannot," he said, adding that youth must be central to the benefits of cooperation.
Meanwhile, Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia's ties with Asean had always been anchored in universally recognised norms of international law and mutual interests.
He described the strategic partnership as an important stabilising factor in the Asia-Pacific region, especially at a time of global geopolitical turbulence, and said it contributed to the development of a balanced security architecture.
"The results of our cooperation are already clear. We have created a solid legal framework and a broad network of joint mechanisms.
"New dialogue platforms have been launched, and practical cooperation has expanded in areas such as security, trade, investment, energy, agriculture, digitalisation, science and technology, and tourism," he said.
He expressed hope that the summit would serve as a platform for constructive and productive exchanges.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WCT secures RM529mil mixed-use project in Abu Dhabi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: WCT Holdings Bhd has secured a construction contract worth about RM529 million (AED479 million) in Abu Dhabi, marking another milestone in the company's expansion in the United Arab Emirates (UAE).
WCT's subsidiary WCT International Sdn Bhd together with UAE-based Construction General Contracting House Ltd (CGCH)  accepted a subcontract award to undertake a major mixed-use development project in Abu Dhabi.
The subcontract was awarded by CGCH to a 50:50 unincorporated joint venture formed between WCT International and CGCH.
Following the award, WCT International and CGCH plan to establish an incorporated joint venture company to execute the formal subcontract agreement and undertake the project works.
The project, known as Yas Living Main Works Package, is being developed by Aldar Development LLC and comprises the construction of three 12-storey commercial and residential buildings across two phases, including basement levels.
Under the scope of works, the joint venture will carry out non-foundational structural works, architectural works, mechanical, electrical and plumbing installations, as well as external works.
Foundation-related works, including piling and excavation, will be undertaken by other contractors.
The subcontract works are expected to commence in the third quarter of 2026 and be completed within 972 days from the commencement date.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia Airports names Azmil Khalid as chairman, extends MD's tenure
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) has appointed Tan Sri Dr Azmil Khalid as its new chairman effective June 18.
Azmil succeeds Dr Nungsari Ahmad Radhi, who stepped down after more than two years as chairman following his appointment as a director on the board of Khazanah Nasional Bhd.
MAHB said Azmil brings more than three decades of experience in corporate leadership, infrastructure development and international business.
He previously served as president and chief executive officer of The AlloyMtd Group and ANIH Bhd between 2011 and 2017, and held senior leadership roles at MTD Capital Bhd.
Azmil is currently group chairman of AFA Group and president and CEO of AFK Group.
He also chairs UEM Edgenta Bhd and several other entities.
The board expressed its appreciation to Nungsari for steering MAHB through a key transition period, including its landmark privatisation exercise.
During his tenure, MAHB was delisted from Bursa Malaysia in February 2025 and became a private entity under the Gateway Development Alliance consortium.
The company said Nungsari also oversaw critical operational and structural milestones, including major capital projects and terminal expansion initiatives at Penang and Kota Kinabalu international airports.
"Dr. Nungsari brought an invaluable perspective during his tenure to balance between the goup's commercial focus post-privatisation and its national duty to manage Malaysia's vital aviation infrastructure," Malaysia Airports said.
The board also announced that Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani's tenure as managing director has been extended for another two years, effective Aug 1.
MAHB said the extension aims to maintain leadership continuity and support the execution of its strategic initiatives.
"The board aims to strengthen organisational stability and ensure uninterrupted execution of MAHB's core strategic initiatives, towards positioning Malaysia as the most connected country in Asia Pacific," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US media slam Trump's 'spin job' over Iran war deal
Article:
WASHINGTON: Pre-war objectives abandoned, Iran's power bolstered, tens of billions of dollars guzzled away: US media appear unanimous in condemning Donald Trump's concessions to Iran in a deal meant to end the war.
The US president put his signature to a memorandum of understanding on Wednesday at a candlelit dinner outside Paris, aiming to draw a line under the war which has engulfed the Middle East and rocked the global economy.
But Trump's return to the United States on Thursday is set to bring him back to reality, with a barrage of criticism expected from both opponents and supporters of the war.
"The White House agreed to this ceasefire extension that met none of its prewar objectives while providing enormous financial concessions to Tehran," said US TV network MS NOW.
"Now, the administration is desperately trying to argue otherwise. Quite simply, Trump got played by the Iranians, and no one is buying his spin job."
"As a first step, Islamic Republic of Iran will instantly reopen the Strait of Hormuz and the United States of America will immediately lift the naval blockade," Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, which mediated the agreement, said on X.
Under the text, Washington also commits to immediately waive oil sanctions that have crippled Iran's economy.
The deal is only a temporary arrangement meant to give time for starting detailed negotiations on the far more complex issue of long-term control over the nuclear power ambitions of Iran, which Washington has long suspected of harbouring a secret bomb-making programme.
The agreement is "widely seen as the biggest foreign-policy bet of the president's second term," according to the Wall Street Journal, who noted Trump "will face resistance from Iran policy hawks who say the president is giving up far more than he is getting."
Even the signing of the deal appeared to fall into disarray, with the business daily reporting that Trump inked the agreement for a second time on Wednesday night, surprising some of his aides and derailing plans for a signing ceremony later this week.
Fox News, the usually Trump-friendly news channel, cited critics who said the agreement gave Iran "huge financial benefits" without requiring the dismantlement of its nuclear programme.
Once a final agreement is reached on the Islamic republic's nuclear programme, the United States will facilitate the release of a US$300 billion reconstruction fund supported by regional nations, according to the deal.
"President Donald Trump's proposed Iran agreement is drawing sharp criticism from some of his strongest supporters, who argue the deal rewards Teheran before it has agreed to fully dismantle its nuclear program," Fox said.
The network quoted British broadcaster Piers Morgan, who said the deal was "about as far removed from 'unconditional surrender' as any deal in the history of Planet Earth."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: L'Oreal to acquire majority stake in Indian beauty startup Innovist
Article:
PARIS: French cosmetics group L'Oreal will acquire a majority stake in India's Innovist, it said on Thursday, as it looks to step up its share of one of the world's fastest-growing markets.
The deal, which includes rights to buy out the minority shareholders in full, adds local brands, including haircare range Bare Anatomy and Chemist at Play skincare, to its portfolio.
Financial details were not disclosed.
L'Oreal's acquisition of Innovist marks its first purchase of an Indian company in nearly 13 years, since it bought Mumbai-based Cheryl's Cosmeceuticals in September 2013.
"Our investment in this innovative Indian startup is a clear testament to our unwavering commitment to expanding L'Oreal's footprint in India," said CEO Nicolas Hieronimus in a statement.
Hieronimus said earlier this year that the company was "not meeting expectations" in India, which is seeing rapid growth driven by young, affluent, social-media-savvy shoppers with rising disposable income.
The company, which makes about one per cent of its turnover in India, had high single-digit growth in the country last year, but "did not gain a lot of market share, if any", Hieronimus told investors after reporting annual results.
It recently brought in a new CEO to boost its performance.
L'Oreal said Innovist is one of the fastest-growing "science-led and digital-first" personal care companies in India, based on clean formulations and transparent ingredients.
The deal is expected to close in the next few months, subject to regulatory approvals, the company said. It added that the founders and minority shareholders will continue to operate the business in collaboration with L'Oréal India. The brand's portfolio will join L'Oréal's Consumer Products division.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Upko joins GRS, expanding Sabah ruling coalition to six parties
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 18 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) today officially accepted the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) as its newest component party, strengthening the local coalition’s ranks ahead of the next state election.
GRS chairman Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the decision was made unanimously during a GRS supreme council meeting held here today and takes immediate effect.
“We have unanimously agreed to accept Upko as a new component member of GRS.
“This is in line with GRS’ own struggle of ‘Sabah United, Sabah First’ and to strengthen local parties within the coalition,” he told reporters after the meeting.
Upko becomes the sixth component party in GRS after formally applying to join the coalition last month.
Hajiji, who is also Sabah chief minister and Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah president, said Upko’s admission would further strengthen the stability of the state government led by GRS.
He confirmed that Upko’s membership became effective immediately following the supreme council’s decision.
“Starting today, Upko is officially a member of GRS and we will formally notify the party of the decision,” he said.
Asked whether any component parties had expressed concerns over Upko’s entry into the coalition, Hajiji said the decision received unanimous support.
“There were none. Everyone agreed because we want to strengthen GRS. There are no issues or concerns,” he said.
Upko president Datuk Ewon Benedick submitted the party’s application directly to Hajiji last month.
At the time, Ewon said Upko wanted to contribute towards greater unity and cooperation among Sabah-based parties to strengthen efforts to develop the state’s future.
He also said the party remained committed to championing Sabah’s rights under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 and the Federal Constitution.
Just days leading up to the Sabah state election in November last year, Upko controversially announced its exit from the national governing coalition Pakatan Harapan, citing differences over the interpretation and implementation of Sabah’s constitutional rights — particularly the 40 per cent revenue entitlement.
Upko has been campaigning on a “Sabah First” political agenda, which Ewon previously said aligns with GRS’ Sabah-centred approach under the Sabah Maju Jaya development framework.
The other five component parties in GRS are Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah and Parti Cinta Sabah.
Three parties – Sabah STAR, Sabah Progressive Party (SAPP) and Usno – had quit GRS before and after the state election.

Title: ‘No child left behind’: SIS Forum to draft law, push new agency to make ex-husbands pay their dues
Article:
ARA DAMANSARA, June 18 — Advocacy group SIS Forum (Malaysia) said it plans to draft its own proposed Bill to ensure all Malaysians pay the financial support owed to their spouse, former spouse and children, amid statistics on a persistent problem of men failing to pay up when marriages break down.
SIS Forum also wants the Malaysian government to set up a new government agency to help all Malaysians get the financial support that should be paid by their former spouse, saying that this proposed agency should help both Muslims and non-Muslims.
SIS Forum executive director Rozana Isa said the non-governmental organisation is planning to also come up with cost estimates when it prepares a draft of the suggested new law for the government.
“We also need to start drafting a Bill. We can’t wait for the ministry to draft the Bill. The lesson we have learnt is we need to draft the Bill ourselves.
“This will be quite a lot of work that we will have to undertake. Because one of the things that is always questioned when you propose a Bill to be tabled is the issue of cost. We have to put in the estimated cost.
“We can no longer just expect to put in a Bill without having a number. Because then nobody is going to take the initiative, so we have to figure out the numbers for that as well. It’s quite a big task which we are quite excited about.
“If we can get this off the ground, I think it will create a change and stem further injustice to our children who will also become parents, wives and mothers themselves down the road,” she said today at the launch of SIS Forum’s 10-year report of its free legal advice service Telenisa’s statistics and findings.
As part of SIS Forum’s long-term plan with a timeline of three years or more, the new federal law it wants pushed for drafting is the Spousal and Child Maintenance Agency Bill to strengthen the enforcement of the rights of “both Muslim and non-Muslim women” to maintenance or financial support from men.
For this proposed new law, SIS Forum said the relevant stakeholders would be the Legal Affairs Division (BHEUU) in the Prime Minister’s Department, the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim), Syariah courts and the Attorney General’s Chambers’ drafting division.
SIS Forum said cost estimates must be included in its proposed Bill so the government can properly assess the financial commitment needed to enforce maintenance rights. — Picture by Choo Choy May .
How big is the unpaid child support problem? (At least 4,800)
Rozana today unveiled Telenisa’s 10-year data based on the 4,761 individuals, about 90 per cent of whom are women, who had asked for help and legal information, with a total of 2,110 cases related to children.
During the 2016 to 2025 period, almost half of those 2,110 cases were about Muslim men’s financial support for children: 633 cases of unpaid child maintenance, and 367 cases involving child maintenance.
So that is 1,000 cases in total.
“Therefore it can be concluded that divorce has a significant impact on child maintenance, particularly involving arrears and child maintenance, which together total 1,000 cases (47.4 per cent). This shows that nearly half of child-related issues are closely linked to financial responsibilities towards children after family breakdown,” the Telenisa 10-year report said.
Rozana also cited the Malaysian government’s December 4, 2025 written parliamentary reply to Bangi MP Syahredzan, where the Department of Syariah Judiciary Malaysia’s (JKSM) Family Support Division (BSK) recorded 4,886 cases of Muslims breaching court orders to pay child maintenance during a five-year period from 2020 to 2024.
Rozana acknowledged that JKSM’s BSK does try to help address cases of Muslim women and children who face unpaid maintenance issues, but said it might need more resources than it currently has.
Proposed solution: A new govt agency to resolve child maintenance for all Malaysians
“One of the reasons why SIS is calling for establishment of a federal Child Support Agency, so that no children would be left in these kind of situations anymore,” Rozana said.
Rozana said SIS Forum is not just asking for Muslim children to be able to get child maintenance, but wants this agency to also help ensure non-Muslim children do not face problems of unpaid child support.
SIS Forum is proposing that the Malaysian government set up this special national-level agency to also cover issues of spouse maintenance.
“This agency will manage and monitor maintenance payments, including an automatic salary deduction system, access to a maintenance order database, and legal action against non-compliance,” it said in its proposal.
The relevant stakeholders for this proposal will be the Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development; Jakim; the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam); the civil courts and Syariah courts, SIS Forum said.
Recommended reading:
From salary deductions to credit blacklists: Ideas to enforce child support in Malaysia

Title: Malaysia jumps eight spots to 15th in IMD World Competitiveness Ranking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Malaysia has jumped eight spots in the 2026 International Institute for Management Development (IMD) World Competitiveness Ranking, rising to the 15th spot among 70 economies, marking its best ranking in recent years.
Last year, Malaysia advanced 11 places to 23rd among 69 economies, up from the 34th place out of 67 economies in 2024.
The ranking was based on four pillars, including the country’s economic performance, government efficiency, business efficiency and infrastructure.
According to IMD, improvements across all four pillars contributed to Malaysia’s stronger standing this year.
Malaysia ranked fourth globally in economic performance, while government efficiency rose 11 spots to the 14th, business efficiency advanced 16 spots to the 16th, and infrastructure also improved by two places to 33rd.
Among the sub-factors, Malaysia’s domestic economy ranking improved four places to 11th, while international trade rose one spot to the fifth position.
International investment also recorded a strong improvement, climbing seven places to the 19th position. — Bernama

Title: Court cuts Guan Eng’s defamation damages against Muhyiddin from RM1.05m to RM450,000
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 18 — The Court of Appeal here today reduced the general damages awarded to DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng from RM1.05 million to RM450,000 in a defamation suit filed against Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin relating to the revocation of tax exemption for Yayasan Albukhary.
A three-member panel comprising Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid and Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar made the ruling after allowing part of Muhyiddin’s appeal against the Kuala Lumpur High Court’s decision.
In the unanimous verdict, Justice Azhahari said after examining the three impugned statements dated March 9, 11, and 12, 2023, the High Court was correct in finding that the statements were defamatory and there was no reason to disturb this finding of fact.
“On the issue of quantum, having considered the pattern of events, and the evidence adduced at trial, we are of the opinion that the general damages awarded are manifestly high.
“Accordingly, this court awarded RM150,000 for each of the three statements leading to RM450,000. We therefore substitute the sum of RM1.05 million with RM450,000,” he said.
The panel also set aside the High Court’s decision in awarding RM150,000 for aggravated damages and RM150,000 for exemplary damages to Lim.
However, the court granted Lim’s cross appeal on interest and ordered that interest be paid at the rate of five per cent per annum from the date of judgment until full settlement.
The panel ordered Muhyiddin to pay RM5,000 in costs to Lim, while the former Penang chief minister was ordered to pay RM30,000 in costs to the former prime minister.
On March 27, 2023, Lim filed a lawsuit against Muhyiddin for allegedly making three defamatory Facebook statements on March 9, 11, and 12, 2023, involving him in the tax exemption issue.
Reports on the matter were subsequently published in several newspapers and news portals.
In November 2024, the High Court allowed Lim’s suit and ordered Muhyiddin to pay RM350,000 for each of the three statements leading to RM1.05 million in general damages, RM150,000 for aggravated damages and RM150,000 for exemplary damages. — Bernama

Title: Sabah names Zainudin Aman as new state secretary
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 18  — The Sabah government has approved the appointment of Datuk Zainudin Aman as the new State Secretary, effective June 29.
Zainudin, who is currently the Sabah State Public Service director-general, will succeed Datuk Seri Safar Untong, whose tenure ends on June 28.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the appointment was expected to further strengthen the Sabah civil service and enhance its competitiveness in driving the state’s administrative transformation agenda.
“This is important to ensure continued progress and excellence, while enabling government policies to be implemented effectively for the prosperity of the people and the state,” he said in a statement today.
Meanwhile, Hajiji expressed the state government’s highest appreciation and gratitude to Safar for his outstanding, dedicated, prudent and integrity-driven service since assuming the post in 2019.
Safar was also appointed chairman of Tanjung Aru Eco Development Sdn Bhd (TAED), a government-linked company under the Chief Minister’s Department, effective June 1. — Bernama

Title: Higher fines, tougher crackdown on ‘mat rempit’ and ‘tonto’ under Road Transport Act amendment to be tabled next week, says Anthony Loke
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) will be tabled at the Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament next week.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the proposed amendments cover 11 scopes involving 42 clauses, including the introduction of penalties for illegal racing and “tonto” activities, as well as higher fines for serious offences,
Harian Metro
reported today.
He said this round of amendments is the most comprehensive in recent years, following the last revision in 2020 which increased penalties for drunk driving offences.
“This is the first amendment to be brought this year, and another amendment is expected to be tabled in the Parliament session at the end of the year.
“The second amendment (at the end of the year) will focus on proposals for a compensation mechanism for victims of accidents involving drunk drivers, drug-positive drivers or reckless driving, to ensure justice for victims,” he said during a special media briefing at the Railway Assets Corporation (RAC) here today.
Loke said one of the key focuses of the amendments is strengthening the digital transformation of the Road Transport Department (JPJ) to reduce reliance on conventional enforcement methods.
He said technologies such as the Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) system would allow enforcement officers to automatically detect road tax status, insurance coverage and vehicle records without stopping vehicles at roadblocks.
“We want smarter and more efficient enforcement, and to reduce direct interaction between officers and road users.
“This approach will not only improve enforcement effectiveness, but also reduce negative perceptions and integrity issues often associated with roadside inspections,” he said.
He said the amendments also aim to curb illegal racing activities and address “tonto” interference, where individuals tail or obstruct enforcement officers.
“We want clearer legal provisions so action can be taken against any party that interferes with or disrupts enforcement duties.
“In addition, the amendments also cover cross-border mobility and other issues that require alignment with current developments,” he said.
On proposals to raise the maximum compound from RM300, Loke said the increase would not apply to all offences, but only selected serious and repeat violations.
He said the current compound rate is no longer seen as an effective deterrent for some road users.
However, if approved, implementation will not be immediate.
“We are looking at a transition period of at least two years before the new rate is enforced.
“The aim is to give the public time to adjust and ensure the implementation is carried out in an orderly manner,” he said.
He said the Bill will be tabled for first reading on Monday, followed by debate at second reading the next day.
He added that the proposed amendments had already undergone scrutiny by the Parliamentary Special Select Committee, involving both government and opposition representatives, before being finalised for tabling in the Dewan Rakyat.

Title: Johor DoE detects air pollution linked to suspected illegal oil dumping at Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort
Article:
PASIR GUDANG, June 18 — The Johor Department of Environment (DoE) has detected air pollution that was believed to be caused by oil residue following an inspection in Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort here.
The DoE team, from the Pasir Gudang branch, were alerted on the matter after worried resident detected a chemical odour since last Saturday.
Johor DoE director Mohd Famey Yusoff said the department’s enforcement team and the Taman Pasir Putih police were despatched to the affected area following the complaints.
He said the team conducted an investigation and also monitoring at the location.
“We found that there was a strong odour resembling the smell of oil and traces of black oil residue found at the main drain of the residential area.
“Further checks found evidence of waste oil dumping at a house near the location of the incident, which is believed to be the cause of the odour pollution,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Famey said the individuals concerned have been identified and their statements taken by DoE investigators.
“Immediate clean-up action was later carried out by contractors appointed by the Pasir Gudang City Council (MBPG).
“If the investigation results in a release, spill or pollution that violates the provisions under the Environmental Quality Act 1974 (Act 127), appropriate enforcement action will be taken,” he said.
Mohd Famey said all parties are reminded not to dispose of any oil, chemicals and scheduled wastes into drains and public drainage systems.
He said this can cause environmental pollution and affect the health and well-being of local residents.
“DoE is still conducting investigations to identify the type and source of the oil residue,” he said.

Title: No more warnings: Selangor JPJ to haul vehicle owners to court over rentals to foreigners in the state
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ) has warned that local vehicle owners found renting out their vehicles to foreigners in the state will no longer be given warnings and will instead be taken straight to court.
Selangor JPJ deputy director Datuk Ahmad Kamarunzaman Mehat said motorcycles were among the most commonly rented vehicles, with daily, weekly and monthly arrangements offered at set rates, according to a report published by Kosmo! Online today.
He said foreigners typically use the rented vehicles for business activities such as selling food, transporting gas cylinders and scrap metal.
“The actions not only endanger road users but also violate the law. Local owners involved will be brought to court without compromise,” he told a press conference at the Selangor JPJ office in Padang Jawa here.
He said 239 foreigners had been taken action against under the Foreign Driver Operation (Ops Pewa) in Selangor from January until yesterday.
